title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE RURAL HEALTH UNIT IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF LUPI, PROVINCE OF CAMARINES SUR, INTO A FIVE-BED CAPACITY HEALTH INFIRMARY, TO BE KNOWN AS THE LUPI HEALTH INFIRMARY, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 30106 / 88 OG No. 27, 4295 (July 6, 1992)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7574, May 26, 1992 ]

AN ACT CONVERTING THE RURAL HEALTH UNIT IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF LUPI, PROVINCE OF CAMARINES SUR, INTO A FIVE-BED CAPACITY HEALTH INFIRMARY, TO BE KNOWN AS THE LUPI HEALTH INFIRMARY, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. The rural health unit in the Municipality of Lupi, Province of Camarines Sur, is hereby converted into a five-bed capacity health infirmary, to be known as the Lupi Health Infirmary.

SEC. 2. The amount necessary to carry out the provisions of this Act shall be included in the General Appropriations Act of the year following its enactment into law and thereafter.

SEC. 3. This Act shall take effect upon its approval.

Approved,

(Sgd.) NEPTALI A. GONZALES

(Sgd.) RAMON V. MITRA

President of the Senate

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on September 4, 1991 and January 30, 1992, respectively.

(Sgd.) ANACLETO D. BADOY, JR.

(Sgd.) CAMILO L. SABIO

Secretary of the Senate

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved: May 26, 1992

(Sgd.) CORAZON C. AQUINO

President of the Philippines
